claim: Context engines enable agents to search across unified knowledge and entity-relate information (connecting Notion docs to pull requests to Jira IDs) to avoid satisfaction-of-search bias where agents stop at the first answer they find
actionable: When building AI agents that need accurate information, implement a unified knowledge graph that allows cross-entity relationships before exposing data to agents, preventing premature stopping at the first result found
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Brandon Waselnuk: Building the Context Engine AI Agents Need

claim: Token optimization is critical: compress relevant context data down to the smallest amount while maintaining high signal so agents receive tightly-scoped, useless-information-free prompts
actionable: After retrieving all relevant context, apply aggressive summarization and filtering to remove noise before passing to agent context windows; focus on signal-to-noise ratio optimization
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Brandon Waselnuk: Building the Context Engine AI Agents Need

claim: Connecting many MCPs (Model Context Protocol) alone does not solve context problems; agent choice of what to call introduces additional probabilism into an already-probabilistic LLM, degrading reliability
actionable: Avoid relying on agents to independently choose which MCPs or tools to use; instead, pre-filter and provide only the most relevant tools for a specific task to reduce decision complexity
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Brandon Waselnuk: Building the Context Engine AI Agents Need

claim: Cost of bad context compounds with every step toward autonomy: tab-complete has low cost if wrong, but autonomous overnight agents waste massive tokens if context is poor, and agents make worse subsequent choices when given bad initial context
actionable: Prioritize context quality and validation as autonomy increases; measure context quality improvements by tracking token efficiency and task success rates, not just completion time
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Brandon Waselnuk: Building the Context Engine AI Agents Need

claim: Larger context windows alone do not guarantee better agent behavior; inflating context with noise typically causes worse decisions than smaller, high-signal contexts
actionable: When expanding context windows, prioritize quality curation over quantity; establish metrics for measuring signal-to-noise ratio in context and test agent decisions before and after context expansion
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Brandon Waselnuk: Building the Context Engine AI Agents Need

claim: Quality of agent planning directly impacts downstream execution: higher-quality initial planning with hydrated context leads to better subsequent choices through a waterfall effect (better context → better first choice → better next choices)
actionable: Invest heavily in prompt engineering for initial planning stages; use agents to refine plans before execution begins, as this multiplies quality improvements across all downstream steps
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Brandon Waselnuk: Building the Context Engine AI Agents Need

claim: Resolution of conflicts across knowledge sources (e.g., documentation says do A but Slack thread from CTO says do B) requires understanding who authored the information and whether their expertise domain matches the use case
actionable: When building context engines, track authorship and expertise domains; weight information by author credibility in their domain (e.g., CTO weight > junior dev on architecture questions) to resolve contradictions
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Brandon Waselnuk: Building the Context Engine AI Agents Need

claim: Personalization is critical for context retrieval: the same query (e.g., implement Zendesk) has different relevant context based on who is asking, what team they work on, and what they are trying to build
actionable: Implement user/team/project scoping in context retrieval systems; filter knowledge graphs by organizational context before returning results rather than treating all queries as identical
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Brandon Waselnuk: Building the Context Engine AI Agents Need

claim: Context engines should be queryable at runtime by agents; static curated docs rot and require maintenance, while live context engines answer agent questions as tasks execute, enabling long-running autonomous agents
actionable: Replace static documentation with queryable knowledge APIs that agents can call during execution; implement version control and freshness indicators for all context data
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Brandon Waselnuk: Building the Context Engine AI Agents Need

claim: Freshness and staleness of context is critical: cached answers decay immediately as teams ship PRs, making real-time context computation necessary rather than answer caching
actionable: Avoid caching final answers from context engines; instead cache only raw data and compute answers on-demand to maintain freshness as code and organizational state changes
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Brandon Waselnuk: Building the Context Engine AI Agents Need

claim: Generated code quality improves significantly (80% faster execution, 50% less token cost) when agents have rich, hydrated context about organizational architecture, team decisions, and code patterns versus agents working with code alone
actionable: Measure baseline agent performance on tasks without context, then incrementally add organizational context (PRs, design docs, Slack threads) and track improvement in token efficiency and execution time
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Brandon Waselnuk: Building the Context Engine AI Agents Need
